text: The video begins with an exploration of the left chest anatomy. The aortic arch is visible, with the superior and inferior pulmonary veins located on its middle side. The pulmonary ligament is divided to expose the inferior pulmonary vein. The fissure is divided, revealing branches of the pulmonary artery leading to the upper lobe and lingula. Lifting the lung exposes the pericardium, phrenic nerve, internal mammary vessels, sympathetic chain, thoracic inlet, and subclavian vessel.
